exercises.title: Trace Comparator compare return
exercises.scenario: Understand what compare() return value means.
hints: b - a: when b > a, result is positive → b comes BEFORE a
So larger numbers come first → descending order

exercises.title: Write a Comparator for descending sort
exercises.scenario: Practice Comparator syntax and reverse logic.
exercises.instructions: Fill in the blanks to sort Strings by length DESCENDING (longest first).

blanks.hint: For descending, put the 'larger should come first' argument first

hints: Descending: compare(b, a) not compare(a, b)
Integer.compare(b.length(), a.length()) returns positive when b.length > a.length
Positive → a comes after b → b (longer) comes first

exercises.title: Sort strings alphabetically with Comparator
exercises.instructions:
Given String[] cities, write a Comparator to sort alphabetically and print.

Expected output:
[Bengaluru, Chennai, Hyderabad, Mumbai]
hints: Comparator<String> with compare(String a, String b)
For alphabetical: a.compareTo(b) — String's built-in comparison
Arrays.sort(cities, comparator)

exercises.title: Sort employees by salary descending
exercises.scenario: Comparator for domain object — descending order.
exercises.instructions:
Create simple `Employee` class (name, salary). Write Comparator to sort by salary DESCENDING. Sort and print names with salaries.

Expected output:
Aisha: 95000.0
Raj: 85000.0
Priya: 72000.0
hints: Descending salary: Double.compare(b.salary, a.salary)
Anonymous class implementing Comparator<Employee>
Arrays.sort(staff, bySalaryDesc)

exercises.title: Fix reversed Comparator direction
exercises.scenario: Common mix-up between ascending and descending arguments.
exercises.instructions:
This sorts descending but should be ascending (cheapest first). Fix it.

Expected output:
Mouse: 599.0
Keyboard: 1999.0
Monitor: 18999.0

bugs.issue: Double.compare(b.price, a.price) gives descending order. Should be a before b for ascending.

hints: Ascending: Double.compare(a.price, b.price) — a comes before b when a.price < b.price
Descending: Double.compare(b.price, a.price) — b comes before a when b.price > a.price
Currently: Double.compare(b, a) → descending. Fix: swap to (a, b)

exercises.title: Named comparator class
exercises.scenario: Reusable comparator as a named class.
exercises.instructions:
Create `Student` class (name, grade). Create a NAMED class `GradeComparator implements Comparator<Student>` that sorts by grade descending.

Expected output:
Aisha: A
Raj: B
Priya: C

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Aisha: A
Raj: B
Priya: C

hints: Named class: class GradeComparator implements Comparator<Student>
Character.compare(a.grade, b.grade) — 'A' < 'B' in ASCII, so ascending gives A first
Arrays.sort(students, new GradeComparator())

exercises.title: Multi-criteria Comparator
exercises.instructions:
Sort `Employee` objects: PRIMARY by department (A-Z), SECONDARY by salary DESCENDING within same department.

Expected output:
Aisha (Engineering): 95000.0
Priya (Engineering): 72000.0
Raj (Sales): 85000.0
Vikram (Sales): 68000.0
hints: Compare department first with a.dept.compareTo(b.dept)
If deptCmp != 0, return it (different departments → department order wins)
If deptCmp == 0, compare salary descending: Double.compare(b.salary, a.salary)

exercises.title: Multiple Comparators on same class
exercises.scenario: One class, multiple sort options selected at runtime.
exercises.instructions:
Create `Book` with title, author, year, rating (double).

Create three Comparators: BY_TITLE (alphabetical), BY_YEAR_DESC (newest first), BY_RATING_DESC (highest first).

Sort and print each way.

Expected output:
--- By title ---
Clean Code
Dune
Foundation
--- By year (newest first) ---
Clean Code (2008)
Dune (1965)
Foundation (1951)
--- By rating (highest first) ---
Dune: 9.1
Foundation: 8.9
Clean Code: 8.7

test_cases.expected_stdout:
--- By title ---
Clean Code
Dune
Foundation
--- By year (newest first) ---
Clean Code (2008)
Dune (1965)
Foundation (1951)
--- By rating (highest first) ---
Dune: 9.1
Foundation: 8.9
Clean Code: 8.7

hints: Three static Comparators as constants
Copy array before each sort: Arrays.copyOf(books, books.length)
BY_YEAR_DESC: Integer.compare(b.year, a.year) — newer year first
BY_RATING_DESC: Double.compare(b.rating, a.rating)

exercises.title: Comparator-driven sort selector
exercises.scenario: Map of comparators, selected by string key — real web app pattern.
exercises.instructions:
Create a simple sort system: a static Map (hint: use String→Comparator lookup). Given `Product` (name, price), support sort keys: 'name', 'price_asc', 'price_desc'.

Note: since Maps aren't formally covered yet, implement as a helper method `getComparator(String key)` returning the right Comparator.

Expected output:
Sorted by name:
Keyboard,Monitor,Mouse
Sorted by price_asc:
Mouse,Keyboard,Monitor
Sorted by price_desc:
Monitor,Keyboard,Mouse

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Sorted by name:
Keyboard,Monitor,Mouse
Sorted by price_asc:
Mouse,Keyboard,Monitor
Sorted by price_desc:
Monitor,Keyboard,Mouse

hints: getComparator(String key) returns different Comparator based on key
switch/if-else on the key string
Each case returns an anonymous Comparator
Real web apps: map the HTTP query param sort_by to the Comparator

exercises.title: Trace multi-criteria sort
exercises.scenario: Predict the result of a two-level sort.
hints: Primary sort: category A-Z (A before B)
Secondary: price ascending within same category
A group: 20, 40. B group: 10, 30

exercises.title: Comparable AND Comparator on same class
exercises.scenario: Natural order plus additional sort options.
exercises.instructions:
Create `Movie` implementing `Comparable<Movie>` (natural sort: by year ascending). Also create static Comparators: BY_TITLE and BY_RATING_DESC.

Demonstrate all three sorts.

Expected output:
--- Natural (year) ---
Godfather (1972): 9.2
Pulp Fiction (1994): 8.9
Interstellar (2014): 8.6
--- By title ---
Godfather
Interstellar
Pulp Fiction
--- By rating (highest) ---
Godfather: 9.2
Pulp Fiction: 8.9
Interstellar: 8.6

test_cases.expected_stdout:
--- Natural (year) ---
Godfather (1972): 9.2
Pulp Fiction (1994): 8.9
Interstellar (2014): 8.6
--- By title ---
Godfather
Interstellar
Pulp Fiction
--- By rating (highest) ---
Godfather: 9.2
Pulp Fiction: 8.9
Interstellar: 8.6

hints: Comparable: compareTo by year ascending (Integer.compare(this.year, other.year))
Comparators defined as static final inside Movie class
Arrays.sort(nat) uses Comparable; Arrays.sort(copy, comparator) uses Comparator

exercises.title: instanceof safe downcast pattern
exercises.scenario: When instanceof is appropriate — type-specific action after polymorphic call.
exercises.instructions:
Create abstract class `Vehicle` with abstract `describe()`. Subclasses `Car` (numDoors) and `Motorcycle` (hasSidecar). 

Write `printDetails(Vehicle v)` that:
1. Calls describe() polymorphically (no instanceof needed)
2. If it's a Car, also prints doors
3. If it's a Motorcycle, also prints sidecar info

Expected output:
Red Car — 4 doors
Blue Motorcycle — no sidecar
exercises.starter_code:
// Abstract Vehicle, Car, Motorcycle
// printDetails uses instanceof only for type-specific extras

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Red Car — 4 doors
Blue Motorcycle — no sidecar

hints: describe() is polymorphic — no instanceof needed for it
instanceof only for the TYPE-SPECIFIC extras (numDoors, hasSidecar)
Pattern matching: if (v instanceof Car c) — check and cast in one
This is appropriate instanceof use: extras that don't belong in Vehicle

exercises.title: Leaderboard with configurable sort
exercises.scenario: Real leaderboard where sort order is selectable.
exercises.instructions:
Build `Player` (name, score, level, country). `Leaderboard` class with Player[] and:
- `rank(Comparator<Player> cmp)` sorts and returns ranked list
- Static Comparators: BY_SCORE_DESC, BY_LEVEL_DESC, BY_NAME

Print top 3 for each sort.

Expected output:
=== By Score ===
1. Aisha: 9500 pts
2. Raj: 8200 pts
3. Priya: 7800 pts
=== By Level ===
1. Aisha: lvl 12
2. Vikram: lvl 10
3. Raj: lvl 9
=== By Name ===
1. Aisha
2. Priya
3. Raj

test_cases.expected_stdout:
=== By Score ===
1. Aisha: 9500 pts
2. Raj: 8200 pts
3. Priya: 7800 pts
=== By Level ===
1. Aisha: lvl 12
2. Vikram: lvl 10
3. Raj: lvl 9
=== By Name ===
1. Aisha
2. Priya
3. Raj

hints: rank(Comparator cmp) copies players array then sorts
Three static Comparators on Leaderboard
Print top 3 from each ranked array
By Name: alphabetical — Aisha, Priya, Raj (Anita and Vikram are 4th/5th)

exercises.title: Polymorphism design: replace instanceof chain
exercises.scenario: Refactor bad instanceof usage into proper polymorphism.
exercises.starter_code:
// Refactor the instanceof chain into a polymorphic design
// Abstract Taxable with computeTax(), three concrete subclasses

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Income tax: 30000.0
Capital gains: 5000.0
Freelance tax: 3320.0

hints: Extract computeTax() into abstract method on Taxable base class
Each subclass overrides it with the specific formula
No instanceof chain needed — polymorphism handles dispatch

exercises.title: Full OOP design: multi-sorted product feed
exercises.scenario: Synthesis — Comparable, Comparator, interface, polymorphism all together.
exercises.instructions:
Build a product recommendation feed:

1. `Recommendable` interface: `double relevanceScore()`, default `boolean isHighRelevance()` → score >= 8.0

2. `Product` implementing `Comparable<Product>` (by relevanceScore desc) and `Recommendable`:
   - Fields: name, category, price, relevance

3. `Feed` class: Product[] (max 20), static Comparators: BY_PRICE_ASC, BY_CATEGORY, BY_RELEVANCE_DESC
   - `getHighRelevance()` returns only products with isHighRelevance()
   - `sortedBy(Comparator<Product> cmp)` returns sorted copy

In main:
- Add 5 products
- Print high-relevance products
- Print sorted by price

Expected output:
High relevance (>=8.0):
Mouse (9.2)
Keyboard (8.5)

Sorted by price:
USB Hub: ₹499
Mouse: ₹799
Python Book: ₹849
Keyboard: ₹1999
Monitor: ₹18999

test_cases.expected_stdout:
High relevance (>=8.0):
Mouse (9.2)
Keyboard (8.5)

Sorted by price:
USB Hub: ₹499
Mouse: ₹799
Python Book: ₹849
Keyboard: ₹1999
Monitor: ₹18999

hints: Product implements both Comparable and Recommendable
getHighRelevance: count first, build array, sort by relevance desc
Comparators defined as static final on Feed
sortedBy copies then sorts with given comparator
